title: Create an Asset Group
content:
Use this endpoint to create an Asset Group in your Business Center. You need to be an Admin user of the Business Center. # Request **Endpoint** https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/bc/asset_group/create/ **Method** POST **Header** ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token{Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type{Required}|string|The content type of the request.
Allowed value: `"application/json"`. | ``` **Parameters** ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |bc_id {Required} | string | Business Center ID. | | asset_group_name {Required} | string | Asset Group name. | | assets {Required} | object[] | Assets you want to add to the Asset Group. Max size is 200. | #| asset_id {Required} | string| Asset ID. When the asset type (`asset_type`) is `ADVERTISER`, this is the advertiser ID. | #| asset_type| string | Asset type. Currently, the only supported type is `ADVERTISER`. You don't have to specify this field since `ADVERTISER` is used by default.| | members {Required} | object[] | Business Center members you want to grant access to your Asset Group. Max size is 50. | #| member_id {Required} | string| Member ID. | #| asset_roles | map < string, string>| The roles you want to assign to the member according to the asset type.
Key: Asset type. Enum value: `ADVERTISER`.
Value: Assigned role. Enum values:
  • `ADVERTISER_ROLE_ADMIN`:
    Manage ad accounts: Control ad account settings, finances and permissions.
    Manage campaigns: Create and edit ads.
    View performance: Access reports and view ads.
  • `ADVERTISER_ROLE_OPERATOR`:
    Manage campaigns: Create and edit ads.
    View performance: Access reports and view ads.
  • `ADVERTISER_ROLE_ANALYST`:
    View performance: Access reports and view ads.
Default value: `{"ADVERTISER":"ADVERTISER_ROLE_ADMIN"}`. ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code) curl --location --request POST https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/bc/asset_group/create/ --header 'Access-Token: {{Access-Token}}' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data-raw '{ "bc_id": "{{bc_id}}", "asset_group_name": "{{asset_group_name}}", "assets": [ { "asset_id": "{{asset_id_1}}" }, { "asset_id": "{{asset_id_2}}" } ], "members": [ { "member_id": "{{member_id}}", "asset_roles": "{{asset_roles}}" } ] }' (/code) ``` # Response ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |code |number | Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |message |string | Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |data |object| Returned data. | #|asset_group_id |string| ID of the Asset Group that was created. | |request_id|string|The log ID of the request, which uniquely identifies a request. | ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code Success-Response http) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "{{request_id}}", "data": { "asset_group_id": "{{asset_group_id}}" } } (/code) ```
